title: phyllanthus zeylanicus
intro: Phyllanthus zeylanicus, also known as Amla or Nelli, is a tropical plant species native to Sri Lanka and parts of India, belonging to the family Phyllanthaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues, liver problems, and skin conditions. The leaves and fruits of the plant are rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, providing several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Phyllanthus zeylanicus is often prepared as a decoction, juice, or powder for various herbal preparations, such as teas, capsules, and topical creams, to harness its medicinal and health-promoting properties.
